symptoms.symptom_name: Deep, severe, night-predominant bone pain of the tibia refractory to maximum OTC analgesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep aching bone pain, often nocturnal, is the commonest symptomatic presentation of active Paget's.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Primary bone malignancy or bone metastasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe night bone pain in an older adult is a classic red flag requiring exclusion of malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive outward (sabre-shin) bowing of the tibia over ~2 years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bowing of a long bone from remodeling of enlarged, soft pagetic bone is characteristic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A slow structural deformity over years is incompatible with an acute venous thrombosis.
Cranial enlargement indicates polyostotic skull involvement — a distinctive feature of Paget's.
Skull-base pagetic disease compromises cochlear/eighth-nerve function, producing sensorineural hearing loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localized warmth over the affected tibia (hypervascularity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Increased blood flow through actively remodeling bone raises overlying skin temperature.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Warmth is also a DVT/cellulitis feature — must be objectively excluded, not assumed pagetic.

symptoms.symptom_name: No calf swelling, no asymmetric limb circumference

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Deep vein thrombosis of the leg
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of unilateral calf swelling lowers, but does not eliminate, the probability of DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no overlying skin breach/ulcer

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of systemic fever and any portal of entry makes acute bone infection unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sabre-shin deformity with localized bone tenderness and increased skin temperature on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable bowing, bony tenderness and warmth confirm active local disease — a clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right knee crepitus and restricted range of motion (secondary osteoarthritis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Secondary osteoarthritis of the right knee
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Altered biomechanics from a bowed tibia drive secondary osteoarthritis at the adjacent joint.

symptoms.symptom_name: Frontal bossing / enlarged cranial vault on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective cranial enlargement corroborates polyostotic skull involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated (bone-specific / fractionated) serum alkaline phosphatase with normal calcium

loinc_ref.display: Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum fractionated / bone-specific alkaline phosphatase, calcium and vitamin D
investigation_details.expected_result: Isolated elevation of total and bone-specific ALP reflecting high bone turnover; calcium typically normal in uncomplicated disease
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The biochemical hallmark that confirms active Paget's and provides a treatment/monitoring baseline is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A raised bone-specific ALP with normal calcium is the confirmatory biochemical signature of active Paget's.

symptoms.symptom_name: Characteristic radiographic changes (cortical thickening, coarse trabeculation, bowing, 'cotton-wool' skull)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain radiographs of the right tibia/fibula and skull
investigation_details.expected_result: Cortical thickening, coarsened trabeculae and bowing of the tibia; 'cotton-wool' lytic/sclerotic changes of the skull; assessment for any aggressive lytic lesion suggesting sarcoma
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A co-existing or transforming bone malignancy (osteosarcoma) could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Radiographic appearance is characteristic and confirmatory.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Primary bone malignancy or bone metastasis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Films are mandatory to exclude sarcomatous transformation or an underlying tumour.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Nocturnal bone pain + progressive long-bone bowing + increasing skull size + unilateral sensorineural hearing loss + localized bone warmth cluster tightly on active Paget's and should trigger urgent confirmatory ALP + radiographs.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum fractionated / bone-specific alkaline phosphatase, calcium and vitamin D
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms active bone turnover and provides a treatment/monitoring baseline; calcium/vitamin D guide safe bisphosphonate use.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiographs of the right tibia/fibula and skull
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characteristic pagetic changes plus mandatory exclusion of malignant transformation and impending fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Doppler ultrasound of the leg (if DVT cannot be clinically excluded on in-person assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Localized warmth mandates objective DVT exclusion that telehealth cannot provide.
Formal characterisation of the sensorineural hearing loss related to skull involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical D-dimer as a stand-alone DVT rule-out in this context
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A D-dimer in an older adult with an obvious alternative inflammatory/hypervascular explanation is likely to be non-discriminating; proceed to in-person assessment +/- ultrasound instead.
